description:
India Book (IB), II, 70. Fustat, 1146. The draft of a letter from the rabbinical court of Fustat to Maḍmūn b. Ḥasan regarding the estate of Ḥalfon ha-Levi b. Shemaryā (Ibn Jumayhir), who was wrecked near Aden. The rabbinical court of Fustat reports hearing the news of the shipwreck first from gentile witnesses, but they waited to act until the news was confirmed by Maḍmūn himself, and shortly thereafter, three reliable Jewish witnesses also reported on the event. 

Divers salvaged some of the goods from the shipwreck. Some of these goods were seized by the sultan, but some of them were deposited with Maḍmūn b. Ḥasan. The goods were evidently of very high value. The court in Fustat wished to find someone to serve as an agent for the deceased man's goods. The agent's job would have been to transport and take possession of the goods, and settle any challenges or lawsuits with other people with whom the deceased man may have had dealings.

They found a suitable candidate in Avraham b. Yosef (Ibn Baqqāl), and offered him the hefty sum of 80 dīnārs to take on the job. But Ibn Baqqāl refused, and further negotiations could not take place as he was already in the process of leaving. We know from PGPID 5534 that he arrived in Aden in 1146. The court in Fustat tells Maḍmūn that they accept if he has found anyone else to serve as trustee, and also empower him to make an arrangement with Ibn Baqqāl himself if the latter is amenable. (PG) (Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Traders)
description_he: ספר הודו II,70: טיוטת מכתב מבית הדין בפוסטאט למדמון בן חסן על עיזבון חלפון שנטבע ליד עדן, 1146
